name: molfeat
tagline: Reference skill teaching Claude to use the molfeat Python library for molecular featurization (fingerprints, descriptors, pretrained embeddi
verdict: Clean, purely educational library-usage skill with no code execution risk of its own: version-pinned installs, an explicit warning against unsafe pickle caching, and a troubleshooting section. Minor nits only: it points to installing MAP4 from an unpinned third-party GitHub repo, has no 'when not to use' guidance, and runs slightly past the ~500-line budget.

highlights.summary: Line 48 tells the user to install MAP4 from `reymond-group/map4` on GitHub outside PyPI with no version pin or hash check, and recommends conda-forge for some heavy deps — a legitimate but unverifiabl
No explicit 'when NOT to use this skill' guidance (e.g., non-Python 3.9/3.10 environments, non-molecular ML tasks) is stated anywhere.
File is 522 lines, modestly over the ~500-line budget.
